name: North Dakota
examName: North Dakota Real Estate Salesperson Examination

examFacts.notes: Administered by PSI for the North Dakota Real Estate Commission (NDREC). The 100-question national portion and the 40-question state portion are scored separately, needing 70 percent and 75 percent respectively.

intro:
The North Dakota real estate salesperson exam is a harder test than its quiet reputation suggests. The North Dakota Real Estate Commission, known as the NDREC, licenses agents and contracts with PSI to deliver the exam as 140 scored questions split into a 100-question national portion and a 40-question North Dakota state-law portion, with two and a half hours allowed for the national questions and an hour and a half for the state questions. The two portions are scored separately, and you need 70 percent on the national half and 75 percent on the North Dakota half, so a strong national score won't carry you if your grasp of North Dakota law is thin. That gap is exactly why North Dakota-specific preparation matters as much as the national material.

State-specific prep matters more here than almost anywhere else. North Dakota tests its own rules in ways a national course never touches, from the five-member NDREC and the powers it uses to investigate and discipline licensees, to the four agency relationships the state recognizes including the appointed agent a designated broker assigns to each side of an in-house deal, the written agency disclosure that must be a separate signed document handed over at first substantive contact under NDAC 70-02-03, the seller's property disclosure that is triggered whenever a licensed agent is involved and only covers residential buildings of four units or fewer, and the oil and gas, radon, and flood-zone facts that come up across the Bakken and the rest of the state. None of that looks like the material a candidate in a neighboring state studies. A generic real estate practice test won't cut it. You need North Dakota-specific practice questions.

Below are 20 free North Dakota real estate test questions and answers from RealReady's full North Dakota bank. Each tests a specific topic with the correct answer and a plain-English explanation under the card. Read each one, commit to an answer, then reveal. Treat it like a real NDREC practice test, and the ones you miss show you what to focus on next.

Get the RealReady app on iPhone, iPad, or Android for the rest of the North Dakota question bank. The full app includes short articles that walk you through the why behind each topic, detailed explanations on every question, a missed-question drill mode, and progress tracking that shows your per-category accuracy.

faq.q: How many questions are on the North Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: The North Dakota Real Estate Salesperson Examination has 140 scored multiple-choice questions, split into a 100-question national portion and a 40-question North Dakota state-law portion. PSI delivers both portions in one computer session and mixes in five to ten unscored experimental questions that look just like the scored ones, so the time spent on those does not count against your result.

faq.q: What's the passing score for the North Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: The two portions are scored separately. You need 70 percent on the national portion, which is 70 of 100, and 75 percent on the North Dakota portion, which is 30 of 40. If you pass one portion and fail the other you only retake the portion you failed, and you may keep testing for a full year after you register.

faq.q: How much does the North Dakota real estate exam cost?
faq.a: PSI charges $134 to sit the salesperson exam, and that one fee covers both portions whether you take them together or separately. A retake is also $134 for one or both portions. The fee is not refundable or transferable, and it is forfeited if you do not test within one year of paying it.

faq.q: How long is the North Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: Four hours total. PSI allows two and a half hours for the 100-question national portion and an hour and a half for the 40-question North Dakota portion, taken in one sitting. PSI runs North Dakota test centers in Bismarck, Minot, Grand Forks, and Fargo, and your score report prints at the test site before you leave.

faq.q: Who administers the North Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: The North Dakota Real Estate Salesperson Examination is administered by PSI for the North Dakota Real Estate Commission (NDREC). You register and schedule your exam date through PSI.

faq.q: Is the North Dakota real estate exam hard?
faq.a: It is a serious test, and the part that catches people off guard is the 40-question North Dakota portion. The national half covers the same principles taught in every state, but the state questions reward candidates who actually studied North Dakota's agency rules, the NDAC 70-02-03 disclosure requirements, the seller's property disclosure exemptions, and the powers of the NDREC. Most people who struggle leaned too heavily on national material and underprepared on North Dakota law. Honest reps on North Dakota-specific practice questions are the difference between a first-time pass and a $134 retake.

faq.q: What's on the North Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: The 100-question national portion covers property ownership, land-use controls, valuation, financing, agency, contracts, property disclosures, transfer of title, the practice of real estate, property management, and real estate math, with contracts and agency carrying the most weight on PSI's content outline. The 40-question North Dakota portion covers the duties and powers of the NDREC, licensing rules, the statutes that govern advertising, the broker and salesperson relationship, commissions, conflicts of interest, the handling of offers and listings, cooperative transactions, the code of ethics, and the Real Estate Education, Research, and Recovery Fund. Agency and disclosure are the heaviest state topics, so they are worth extra attention.

faq.q: What's the best way to prepare for the North Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: After you finish North Dakota's required pre-license education, the highest-leverage move is grinding North Dakota-specific practice questions with honest feedback on every miss. National principles you can pick up from any course, but the 40-question state portion rewards repetition on NDREC rules, the four agency relationships and the appointed agent, the first-substantive-contact disclosure under NDAC 70-02-03, and the seller's property disclosure exemptions. The RealReady app gives you the full North Dakota question bank plus the national bank, detailed explanations, a missed-question drill mode, and per-category progress tracking so you can see exactly where you are weak.

The 20 North Dakota practice questions on this page are free, no account or signup needed. Unlocking the rest of the North Dakota bank is a single in-app purchase you pay once and own, with no recurring charge and no time limit on access.

faq.q: Does RealReady cover the North Dakota real estate exam?
faq.a: Yes. RealReady has a dedicated North Dakota question bank built for the North Dakota real estate exam, covering the national principles every candidate sees plus the North Dakota law that trips people up, like the four agency relationships including the appointed agent, the written agency disclosure under NDAC 70-02-03, and the seller's property disclosure.

faq.q: Is RealReady a real estate license course?
faq.a: No. RealReady is exam practice, not a pre-license course, so it doesn't count toward North Dakota's required pre-license education hours. Use it after or alongside your coursework to drill realistic practice questions and find your weak spots before exam day.
